lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, the whole gang is there, the guys are watching football, the girls are cooking Thanksgiving dinner.

lines.speaker: The Guys
lines.text: (reacting to a play) Yes! Yes! Yes! Yes! Awww!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, it's your Thanksgiving too, y'know, instead of watching football, you could help.

lines.speaker: The Guys
lines.text: We will. (they don't move)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, Rachel, you wanna put the marshmellows in concentric circles.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No Mon, you want to put them in concentric circles. I want to do this.
Rachel sticks a marshmellow into Monica's nose. Monica takes it out of her nose by closing one nostril, and blowing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Every year.
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know, for once, I am going to sit down and try to watch one of these things. (just as she sits down).

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Halftime.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, who wants to ah, throw the ball around a little, maybe get a little three on three going?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! That would be sooo much fun!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, can I play too? I've never played football, like ever.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great, you can cover Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no, I don't, I don't really wanna play.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on man! You never want to do anything since you and Janice broke up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's not true! I wanted to wear my bathrobe and eat peanut clusters all day. I wanted to start drinking in the morning. Don't say that I don't have goals!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler, you have to start getting over her. All right, if you play, you get some fresh air, maybe it'll take your mind off Janice, and if you don't play, everyone will be mad at you 'cause the teams won't be even. Come on.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, all right, I'll play.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Let's do it! Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you wanna play football?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um, Monica and I aren't supposed to play football.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Says who? Your mom?

lines.speaker: Monica and Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, every, every Thanksgiving um, we used to have a touch football game called the 'Geller Bowl.'

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no, you say that proudly.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Anyway, Ross and I were always captains, and um, it got kind've competitive and one year, Geller Bowl VI, I accidentally broke Ross's nose.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It was soo not an accident. She saw I was about to tag her, so she threw her big fat grandma arm elbow right into my face. And just keep running.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: To score the winning touchdown, by the way.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa, ho, ho, ho, you did not win the game, the touchdown didn't count, because of the spectacularly illegal, oh and by the way savage nose breaking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Chandler and Joey) I won the game.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah! Then how come you didn't get the Geller Cup?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um, there was a Geller Cup?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, it was the trophy you got if you won the game. But our Dad said, 'nobody won that game, ' and he was sick of our fighting, so he took the trophy and.... (pauses to collect himself, as he is on the verge of tears saying this) threw it in the lake.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And was the curse lifted?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Anyway. That's when our Mom said we were not to play football ever again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what, I think we should play a game. I mean come on, it's been twelve years.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can I see you for a second?
they walk over to the sink and discuss it for a moment

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (shouting) Once!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, we're gonna play.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But wait a minute though, how are we gonna get there, though, because my Mom won't let me cross the street.
The Park, the gang is warming up for their football game.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Let's bring it in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait no, honey, honey throw it to me, throw it to me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here you go. (throws her the ball)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (knocking it down instead of catching it) That almost hit me in the face.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, we have to pick captains.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And then Tineals.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, so how do we decide that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, why don't we just bunny up.

lines.speaker: Rachel, Chandler, and Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica and Ross
lines.text: (holding both of their hands above their heads making rabbit ears with their fingers.) Bunny!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, looks like Ross and I are captains. Okay, so um, I bunnied first so that means I get to pick first. Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, I'm your best friend.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sweetie, don't worry you'll get picked. Chandler.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe.
Phoebe kisses Rachel on the cheek, then joins her team.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sweetie, now I pick you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You don't pick me! You're stuck with me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. All right. So let's see, let's play from the trash can, to the lightpost. Right. Two hand touch, we'll kick off.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right people listen, I've got exactly twenty-eight minutes before I have to baste again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! Just like in the pros.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Huddle up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to his team) All right, huddle up, right over here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait for me! Wait for me! Wait for me! Oh cool, this is my first huddle.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, so what do you guys really think of Chandler?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, Phoebe you know what you're doing right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, Joey's gonna catch it, and you and I are gonna block.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What's block?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, I thought you said you know what you're doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I thought you meant in life.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Break.
Chandler is getting ready to kick off, Ross is holding the ball between his foot and finger.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The ball is Janice. The ball is Janice. (goes to kick the ball but kicks Ross's foot instead.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oww!! Son of a...!! Ow! Come on!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sorry. I'm sorry. Y'know what, we're just gonna throw it.
Chandler throws the ball to kick-off.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I got it. (catches the ball)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Go! Go! Go!
Joey runs up field and fakes out Ross and scores a touchdown. His team all celebrates the touchdown.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Score!! 7 to nothing!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (coming over to Ross, who is just getting up) Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, let's go!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Losers walk!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, losers talk!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no, actually losers rhyme.
[cut to later, Ross's team has the ball.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (coming up under center, just like a real quarterback does, and puts his hands between Ross's legs.) Twenty-three!! Seventy-four!! (Ross stands up and looks at him) You wanna go shotgun?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (from the shotgun) Hike!

lines.speaker: Monica and Joey
lines.text: One-Mississippi. Two-Mississippi. Three-Mississippi.
Rachel runs a quick slant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Over here!
Chandler throws her the ball, which she drops.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (proud of her self) I almost caught that one!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Great! Now, the score is 7 to almost 7.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, (to Chandler) this play, I want you to do a down and out to the right. Okay. Break!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, what am I gonna do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You, you go long.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, how long?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Until we start to look very small.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Break!!
[cut to later, Monica's team has the ball.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Set....hike!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: One-Mississippi, two-Mississippi, switch! Switch! Switch!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no, no, no!
Monica throws the ball over Joey's head, it's stopped from rolling away by a very beautiful woman.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Haaaaa! Hey-hey, thanks for stopping our ball.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: (in a foreign accent) You are playing American football?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Wow, your like from a whole other country.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I'm Dutch.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi-hi, I'm Joey.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I'm Margha.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm sorry Dutch, I didn't get that last little bit.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (running up) Hey Joey, do you wanna play football or you wanna.. (sees Marhan) Hi, I'm Chandler.

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: Hello, Chandler.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Her name is Dutch, and also Marklan.

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: Margha.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Mar-klan.

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: Mar-gha.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mar-haaaan.
[cut to Ross and Monica.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on guys! Let's go! Come on, it's second down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, hello, it's third down.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No it's not, it's second.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow, what?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It just amazes me that your still pulling stuff like this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pulling what? It's second down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, it's second down. (turns away) Take all the second downs you need.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I heard that!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I said it loud.
[cut to Chandler, Joey, and Margha.]

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: It is okay, if I stay and watch?

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Why don't you stick around. You can sit right there.
she goes and sits down

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, that went well.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I think so.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I was thinking about ah, asking her for her number.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks man, but I think it makes a stronger statement if I ask for it myself, y'know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whoa-ho, whoa! No, I was thinking about y'know for me, as a part of that whole getting over Janice thing you were talking about.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah, that. All right, means that much to ya, I'll let you have her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks. What, let me have her?! What do mean? Like if you didn't I wouldn't have a shot?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I don't like to say it out loud, but, yeah! Don't feel bad man, we all have our strengths. You're better with numbers and stuff.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Math!! You're giving me math! All right, look y'know what, forget about it, you go for the girl, we'll see who gets her.
[cut to later, in Ross's huddle.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler, I want you to run a post pattern to the left, okay. And sweetie..

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I know, go long. Y'know, it's like all I'm doing is running back and forth from the huddle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well ah, you wanna just stay out there?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can I see that for second.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.
Rachel takes the ball from his hands and bounces it off of Ross's forehead and Chandler catches the rebound.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Hut! Hike!
Chandler runs around behind Ross, who pitches him the ball. Chandler runs upfield, and Joey knocks the ball out of his hands.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fumble!
Joey starts to return the fumble and Chandler grabs Joey's shirt and rips it off of his back.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What the hell's the matter with you?! This is my favourite jersey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well now you have two. Hey, I am good at math.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, that's it. Y'know I was still gonna let you have her. But now, forget about it. Prepare to feel very bad about yourself.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Well, I've been preparing for that my entire life! Or something about you that's mean!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, come on guys, let's go! Tie score, and we're runnin' out of time. Forty-two!! Thirty-eight!! Hike! (the timer sounds as Monica throws the ball to Phoebe.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh I got it!! (catches the ball) Oh! Ew! Broken boob! Ow!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs, run!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Run, Phoebe, run!
Phoebe runs and scores a touchdown.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Touchdown!! Touchdown!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, hello, the buzzer buzzed. It doesn't count.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: After the snap!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Before the snap!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: After!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Before!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now, does it really matter?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, okay, I made a touchdown. It was my first touchdown. So?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh Pheebs, that's great. It doesn't count.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Does so count!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Cheater, cheater, compulsive eater.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what, that's fine, maybe you haven't grown up, but I have.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-ho, okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Dead leg!! (kicks him in the thigh.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ow! Ow! Okay, okay, fine, fine! All right, you wanna win by cheating, go ahead, all right. Phoebe the touchdown does count, you win.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Woo-yay!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Listen, I'm not gonna go through this with you again, okay. Just once I wanna beat when you can't blame it on the broken nose, the buzzer, or the fact that you thought you were getting mono. Let's just call this, tie score and it's halftime.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, first of all, I don't play with cheaters, and second of all, you know I had swollen glands!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what? I'll think you'll play.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh really! Why is that?
[cut to Monica and Rachel's.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because the winner gets this!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The Geller Cup.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is everybody else seeing a troll doll nailed to a two by four?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, good.
Commercial Break
The park, the gang is returning to play the second half of the game.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, where in the hell did you get that?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: When Mom and Dad drove you to the hospital to get your nose fixed, I swam into the lake and fished it out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That cup is mine!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No it's not! You want it, you're gonna have to win it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, so are we not having dinner at all?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on Phoebe, let's go! Come on, it's time to get serious, huddle up. Joey, keep your head in the game.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's hard, y'know, his huddle is closer to Dutch girl.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right look, if I take Chandler out of the running will you be able to focus?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, you just make sure that Chandler catches the ball, I'll take care of the rest.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Break!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Here you go!
Joey throws the ball to Chandler

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler! Chandler!
Chandler catches the ball and starts to run upfield.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Margha) Hi.
just as he gets in front of Margha, Monica comes up and tackles him

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Whoa! Whoa!! Tackled by a girl! Bet ya don't see that everyday, do ya?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa! Whoa! Whoa! What's with the tackling?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! I just touched him and he went over.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, you wanna play rough, we can play rough.
They both stare each other down as we hear 'Let's get ready to r-r-r-rum-ble!!!'
A long football sequence follows.
[Sequence 1: Monica throws the ball over Chandler's head to Joey who catches it for a touchdown, and starts to dance in celebration. Chandler then tackles him, and he starts to dance in celebration.]
[Sequence 2: Monica runs upfield and stops, waiting for a pass. Ross runs over and pulls her pants down, steps in front of her and intercepts the pass.]
[Sequence 3: Chandler throws a pass to Ross, who catches it. Phoebe starts screaming and runs up to him and tries to tackle him. But all she ends up doing is running around his waist and screaming.]

[Sequence 4: Ross hikes the ball to Chandler, and the camera pans down to show Rachel standing deep in the end zone, playing with her gum. Something hits her on the head and she looks up to see where it came from.]
[Sequence 5: Monica hands the ball off to Phoebe, who runs up field and delivers a fore-arm shiver to Chandler, knocking him over and scores the touchdown, and she yells...]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I love this game!!
[cut to Ross who walks up to Rachel who is eating a baked pretzel.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, where'd you get that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I went really long.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Forty-two to twenty-one! Like the turkey, Ross is done!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's no surprise that your winning, 'cause you got to pick first, so you got the better team.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're so pathetic! Why can't you just accept it, we're winning because I'm better than you.
Ross makes a 'Yeah. Right.' sound.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, what a great argument, exhaling! All right, y'know what, I'll prove it to you, okay. I'll trade you Joey for Rachel, and I'll still win the game.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! The guys against the girls? See, that's ridiculous Monica, because I'm only down by three touchdowns.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, then bring it on! Oh, unless of course your afraid you might lose to a bunch of girls.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine, fine, Rachel your with Monica, Joey you're with me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can not believe your trading me!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on Rach, come on. Let's see what's it like to be on a winning team for a change.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you gonna let me play?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right then.
[cut to the guys' team.]

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: (coming over) The game is over, we eat now?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no-no, the game's not over, we're just switching teams.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, Chandler finds me so intimdating that it's better if we're on the same team.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right. Okay, let's play. Let's go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No ah, hold on a second Joe, where do Dutch people come from?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah well, the ah, Pennsylvania Dutch, come from Pennsylvania.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And the other ah, Dutch people, they come on from somewhere near the Netherlands, right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nice try. (to Margha) See the Netherlands is this make believe place where Peter Pan and Tinker Bell come from.

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: Oh, my.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Enough with geography for the insane, okay? Let's play some ball, guys.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa, whoa, no, no, I-I'm not playing with this guy, now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine with me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, y'know what, let's just cut to the chase here. Okay? Heidi, which of my boys do you like?

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: What are you doing? What are you doing? What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: Which do I like?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, y'know for dating, general merriment, taking back to your windmill...

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: Well, if I had to chose right now, which by the way I find really weird, I would have to say, Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait a minute! Wait a minute! She obviously didn't understand the question.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you don't you have Captain Hook explain it to her.

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: I'm sorry, Joey, that is my chose.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You hear that! That is her chose, mister I'll let you have her! I win! You suck! I rule all! A mini-wave in celebration of me!! (does the wave.)

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: I'm now thinking I would like to change my answer to, no one.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wh-what?

lines.speaker: Margha
lines.text: I now find you shallow and um, a dork. All right, bye.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nice going. You just saved yourself a couple months of sex.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what, it doesn't matter, 'cause she picked me. Me! From now on I get the dates and you have to stay home on Saturday nights watching Ready, Set, Cook!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Save the breakthroughs for therapy, okay. The clock is ticking. We have no time, and we are losing, we are losing to girls.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We're not gonna lose to girls.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! It's 42-21!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This sucks, I was just up by that much!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are we playing football or what? Come on you hairy-backed Marries.
[cut to the girls huddle.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We have to do this. We are playing for women everywhere. Okay, just think about every lousy date that you ever had, okay, every guy who kept on the TV while you're making out...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! You dated someone with a glass eye too?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on, okay, come on this is for all womankind. Let's kill 'um!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!!! Kill 'um!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, no, well I want to kill them to, but their boys, y'know how are we gonna beat three boys?
Another football sequence follows
[Sequence 1: Chandler is running past Phoebe with the ball, Phoebe flashes him, he stops and stares dumbfounded at her. Phoebe then runs up and takes the ball away.]

[Sequence 2: Phoebe throws the ball, and it's intercepted by Joey, who starts to run up field. Rachel jumps on his back in order to try and tackle him, but she doesn't slow him down. Monica and Phoebe then both grab her legs in order to stop Joey, who still manages to fight through the tackle and score the touchdown.]
[Sequence 3: Chandler is running with the ball, Phoebe flashes him again, but Chandler covers his eyes, and keeps running. He then runs into a tree at the end of the field.]
[cut to the girls huddle]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, we still have a minute and a half to go, and we're down by two points. Two points.... (she gets interrupted by the guys, who are doing a slow-motion high five.) Phoebe you do a button-hook again. Rachel, you go long.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Come on! Don't make me go long. Use me. They never cover me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, there's a reason.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: God, I'm not lame, okay. I can do something. I can throw, would you let me throw, come on this is my game too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on Mon, let her throw the ball.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right Rachel, you sweep behind, I'll pitch it to you, you throw it down field to Phoebe. All right. Break.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you! Break!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thirty-two! Seventy-one! Hike!
Phoebe snaps the ball to Monica, who pitches back to Rachel.

lines.speaker: The Guys
lines.text: One-Mississippi! Two-Mississippi! Three-Mississippi!

They all rush toward Rachel, who panics and runs away. She runs out of the park and up along the fence, she then comes back into the park and runs past Monica, as she gets to Monica, she throws the ball at Monica, and it hits her in the eye.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm so sorry! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! I'm not okay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry, they were just all coming at me, and I didn't know what to do.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking at the timer) Thirty seconds left on the timer!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, okay, so we get to take that stupid troll thing home!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on! Come on! Hurry! We're running out of time! Huddle up!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Oooh! Oh, this is our last huddle, yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, Phoebe get open. Rachel, go long.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on the verge of tears) Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Break!

In slow motion, Phoebe snaps the ball, Rachel goes long. Joey and Chandler and all over Phoebe, leaving Rachel wide open. Ross starts to rush Monica, who sees Phoebe is double covered, in desperation she throws to Rachel. We see flying through the air, and then Rachel running underneath it, then the ball, then Rachel again, then the ball, then Phoebe, Chandler, and Joey staring at it in shock. Then with the grace of Jerry Rice (no offense to Jerry Rice), Rachel catches the ball, and she stops and spikes the ball. Both Phoebe and Monica erupt in celebration.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (in triumph) I got a touchdown! We did it!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey Rachel, funny thing. Actually, the ah, end zone starts at that pole, so you're five feet short, so we win!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait-wait-wait-wait! So, explain something to me though, if, if nobody tagged Rachel, then isn't the play still going.
they all start to dive for the ball and Monica and Ross grab it at the same time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let go! Let go!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let go! I'm a tiny little woman!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Guys! Guys! Come on! It's Thanksgiving, it's not important who wins or loses. The important thing is, (to Joey) the Dutch girl picked me! Me! Not you! Holland loves Chandler! Thank you, Amsterdam! Good night!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ow!!
Closing Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel, Phoebe, Chandler, and Joey are eating Thanksgiving dinner.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We should defiantly play football more often. Maybe there's a like league we could join or something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Isn't there a national football league.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes. Yes, there is, they play on Sundays and Monday nights.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh shoot! I work Monday nights.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, this stuffing is amazing. Do you think we should bring them some?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: When they're hungry enough, they'll come in.
The park, it's dark outside and Monica and Ross are still fighting over the ball.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let go!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! You let go!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How come it's always us left in the field holding the ball?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know. I guess the other people just don't care enough.
It starts snowing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! It's starting to snow.
They both look up, and watch it start to snow. Then they both start fighting for the ball again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Gimme the this!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let go!
End
